input: The Q2 report shows revenue of 1200000, up 18 percent from Q1. Headcount grew from 45 to 52. Churn fell to 3 percent.
The outage lasted 47 minutes and affected 2 regions. Root cause was a config change at 14:03. 3 services were degraded.
The load test ran 500 concurrent users for 10 minutes. P95 latency was 320ms. Error rate was 0.4 percent. Throughput peaked at 1200 requests per second.
Audit found 14 open vulnerabilities: 2 critical, 5 high, 7 medium. 3 issues were remediated within 48 hours. 11 remain pending.
Sprint 22 closed 38 tickets. Velocity was 84 points, up from 71 last sprint. 3 tickets were carried over. Team size was 6 engineers.
Database migration took 4 hours and moved 2.1 TB of data across 17 tables. Downtime was 12 minutes. 0 rows were lost.
Customer survey results: 1200 responses, NPS score 62, up 8 points from last quarter. Top complaint was slow support response time cited by 340 respondents.
Deployment pipeline ran in 9 minutes. 4 stages completed: lint, test (92 percent pass), build (docker 1.2 GB image), and deploy to 3 nodes.
Revenue rose from 800000 to 1000000 this quarter, a 25 percent increase. Operating margin held at 12 percent.
The team of 8 engineers shipped 3 features across 6 weeks. Two incidents occurred, each resolved within 45 minutes.
Daily active users grew from 12000 to 15000, up 3000. Day-30 retention was 41 percent, up from 38.
The build pipeline runs 1240 tests in 4.2 minutes; the flaky rate dropped to 0.8 percent from 2.1.
The cluster has 6 nodes and 192 GB RAM total, 78 percent utilized at peak; 2 nodes were added last month.
The survey had 540 respondents; 63 percent preferred option A, 29 percent option B, the rest undecided.
Latency p50 is 120 ms, p95 is 480 ms, p99 is 1.2 seconds across 3 regions.
Costs fell from 90000 to 72000 per month after migrating 14 services, a saving of 18000.
